title: - What makes a story feel alive instead of chaotic?
content:
- What makes a story feel alive instead of chaotic?

AI: Format: Conversational intellectual excavation via phone calls, voice memos, shared documents. No deliverables—thinking out loud.

AI: Outcome: "Narrative Dramaturgical Charter" establishing principles for participatory narrative coherence. Future collaborators reference: "Chris Notarnicola and Anthony Padavano solved this problem in 2025."

ANTHONY: Your dramaturgical vocabulary becomes the foundation. Not executing my vision—building the framework together.

---

Phase 2: Prototype Development (Weeks 4–8)

ANTHONY: You choose:

Option A: Adapt Tomb of the Unknowns. We map your existing structure onto participatory parameters. Veterans and family members shape which voices dominate, which memories surface.

Option B: Classical text you've wanted to unpack. Greek tragedy, Shakespeare, whatever. Audience input reshapes thematic emphasis without destroying coherence.

Option C: Create something new as participatory narrative from scratch. 25–40 minutes.
